mnemonics: P.A.R.T. - A set is Partially Ordered if it is Reflexive, Antisymmetric, and Transitive.
keyPoints: A relation R on a set P is a partial order if it is reflexive (aRa for all a), antisymmetric (if aRb and bRa then a=b), and transitive (if aRb and bRc then aRc).
Not every pair of elements needs to be comparable (aRb or bRa). This is what makes it 'partial' and not 'total'.
The set P with this relation, denoted (P, ≤), is called a partially ordered set or poset.
commonMistakes: Assuming all elements must be comparable. In a poset, some elements can be incomparable.
Confusing antisymmetry with asymmetry. Antisymmetry allows for equality (aRb and bRa implies a=b), while asymmetry forbids it (aRb implies not bRa).
